qualitative_analysis.title: Inversis is #6 depositary in Spain with 3.2% market share
qualitative_analysis.body: Inversis custodies €15.3B across 29 gestoras and 263 funds (FI + SICAV). The overall depositary market (FI + SICAV, ex-foreign IICs / FIL / FCR / pensions) totals €445B, dominated by Cecabank (44.7%) and CACEIS (19.5%). Inversis’s position is structurally differentiated: it serves independent boutiques and Andorran-origin private banking gestoras that are excluded from captive bank depositaries.

qualitative_analysis.title: Estimated annual depositary fee revenue: €7.5M at 0.2 bps effective rate
qualitative_analysis.body: The Inversis depositary book generates an estimated €7.55M/yr in depositary fees. The top two fee contributors are ANDBANK WEALTH MANAGEMENT (€1.81M) and MARCH ASSET MANAGEMENT (€0.93M), together representing 36% of total depositary income.

qualitative_analysis.title: Client concentration: top 2 groups account for 51% of AUM and revenue
qualitative_analysis.body: Andbank group (€3.23B, €2.12M fees) and Banca March group (€3.77B, €1.73M fees) together represent 57% of AUM under custody. The Banca March relationship will transition to arm's-length terms upon Euroclear acquisition (August 2026), creating both a repricing opportunity and an account retention priority. Andbank, as the highest-value third-party client, should be treated as a Tier 1 strategic account.

qualitative_analysis.title: €44B addressable market outside current Inversis relationships
qualitative_analysis.body: 47 non-captive gestoras totalling €44B AUM currently use other depositaries. The most valuable non-captive targets include TREA ASSET MANAGEMENT S.G.I.I.C., BESTINVER GESTION, MEDIOLANUM GESTION. Inversis’s competitive advantage in this segment is its combination of regulatory independence, operational breadth (263 funds across 29 gestoras), and — post-Euroclear — tier-1 group backing without conflicting distribution interests.
